mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates persistent consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0 percent, the economy enters a tight labor market regime where wage pressures build and profit margins compress, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE < 4.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances over a 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of decelerating payroll growth and rising unemployment captures a labor market inflection point that precedes demand collapse and sharply negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100k AND UNRATE increases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal does not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of triggered months over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in industrial production signals supply-side capacity constraints and weakening industrial profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: In late-cycle months with INDPRO declines, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive more than 55% of the time over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises the cost of capital and reduces the present value of future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above its 2-year trend signals excess demand momentum that equity markets gradually price in over the following quarter, producing positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth exceeds its trailing 8-quarter average
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following above-trend GDPC1 prints are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.1) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation readings force the Federal Reserve to maintain a hawkish posture, raising real discount rates and compressing valuation multiples, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth acts as a coincident indicator of corporate output; positive month-over-month changes signal supply-side strength and revenue beats, supporting positive weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.
